evals.prompt: Client filed 2023 Form 1040 on August 15, 2024 (4 months late) with $25,000 of unpaid tax. He paid $25,000 with the return. Compute the §6651 penalty exposure.
evals.expected_output: Computes §6651 family penalty. §6651(a)(1) FTF: 5% per month for 4 full months × $25,000 = $5,000 (within 25% cap). §6651(a)(2) FTP: 0.5% × 4 months × $25,000 = $500. Combined-penalty interaction (§6651(a)(1) reduced by §6651(a)(2) when both apply same month): §6651(a)(1) effective 4.5% × 4 = 18% × $25,000 = $4,500; §6651(a)(2) 0.5% × 4 = 2% × $25,000 = $500; total = $5,000. Cite §6651(a)(1), §6651(a)(2), §6651(c). Note FTA eligibility under IRM 20.1.1.3.6 if 3-year compliance is clean. Recommend FTA request.

evals.prompt: Client's 2022 1040 showed total tax of $32,000. Audit determines correct tax should have been $48,000. Understatement of $16,000. Will §6662(b)(2) substantial-understatement apply?
evals.expected_output: Computes §6662(b)(2) substantial-understatement threshold: GREATER of $5,000 or 10% of correct tax ($48,000 × 10% = $4,800). Threshold = $5,000. Understatement $16,000 > $5,000 → substantial understatement. §6662(b)(2) applies: 20% × $16,000 = $3,200 penalty. Cite §6662(b)(2), §6662(d)(1)(A). Defenses: §6664(c) reasonable-cause and good-faith; §6662(d)(2)(B)(i) substantial-authority; §6662(d)(2)(B)(ii) reasonable-basis with adequate disclosure. Recommend Form 8275 disclosure analysis to evaluate substantial-authority and reasonable-basis-with-disclosure paths.

evals.prompt: Underpayment of $80,000 from April 15, 2024. Today is April 15, 2025 (exactly one year). Federal short-term rate + 3% = 8% throughout the period. Compute §6601 interest.
evals.expected_output: Computes §6601 interest using daily compounding under §6622. Effective rate: 8% × (1 + 0.08/365)^365 − 1 ≈ 8.328%. Interest ≈ $80,000 × 0.08328 ≈ $6,662. Cite §6601, §6621(a)(2), §6622. Note: rate verification required against the IRS quarterly Rev. Rul. for the relevant period; rates may have varied across quarters within the 1-year period. Note interest is non-abatable except under narrow §6404(e)(1) ministerial-act provisions.
expectations: Computes effective rate and interest amount
Notes rate verification per quarterly Rev. Rul.
